$comment: GENERATED byte-exact RBS1 golden vectors. Oracle: crates/rill-guest/src/store_net_encode.rs (encode_envelope). Regenerate with RILL_WRITE_STORE_NET_GOLDEN=1 cargo test -p rill-guest golden_vectors_are_locked. Each envelope vector pairs a control plane + segments with the exact on-the-wire bytes; the framing is SYMMETRIC (one vector locks request AND return). The at-cap 1 MiB vector omits envelopeHex for size and locks envelopeLen + structure. Segments: {hex} raw bytes, or {fill,len} a repeated byte. The back-compat vector is raw JSON (kind=plain), asserted NOT to be a frame.

vectors.name: back-compat-no-segments
vectors.note: segment-free value: raw JSON, NOT an RBS1 frame (back-compat invariant)

vectors.name: one-segment
vectors.note: a single byte-stream field hoisted to segment 0

vectors.name: zero-and-ff-bytes
vectors.note: raw 0x00 and 0xFF ride untouched in the segment

vectors.name: multi-segment-nested
vectors.note: nested sentinels in an object and an array; walk order a=0,b[0]=1,b[1]=2

vectors.name: at-cap-1mib-segment
vectors.note: an at-maxSegmentBytes (1 MiB) byte stream of 0xab; envelope hex omitted for size, length + structure locked
vectors.envelopeHexOmitted: length 1048610 exceeds inline cap; length + structure locked by the Rust suite
